title: M8: CI/CD pipeline + production launch
description:
Ship to production.

CI/CD setup:
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml: Node 20 → npm ci → npm run build → rsync over SSH on push to main
- Optional .github/workflows/staging.yml on push to develop → staging path
- Required GitHub secrets: SSH_HOST, SSH_USER, SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, SSH_TARGET_PATH
- Founder generates SSH keypair, adds public key to host authorized_keys, adds 4 secrets to repo

Server config (.htaccess in dist/):
- Force HTTPS
- Cache headers: immutable max-age=31536000 for /\_astro/* (hashed); no-cache for HTML
- Pretty URLs (already handled by Astro build.format: 'directory')
- Custom 404 → /404.html per locale
- gzip/brotli via mod_deflate if available

Launch checklist:
- DNS cutover plan (TTL lowered 24h before)
- Old URL redirect map (legacy Laravel routes → new equivalents) via .htaccess RewriteRule
- Submit sitemap to Google Search Console + Bing Webmaster
- Verify with Rich Results Test for JSON-LD
- Smoke test: home, both locales, 1 case study, /founder, /contact WhatsApp link, /blog
- Tag git release v1.0.0
- Archive Laravel branch as legacy/laravel-cms (force-push if needed)

Post-launch:
- Monitor 24h for 404s via host access logs
- Confirm rsync deploy round-trip < 2 min on push
acceptance_criteria:
- Push to main triggers GitHub Actions, completes < 5 min, site updates within 2 min
- All legacy URLs either resolve or 301 redirect to new equivalents
- HTTPS enforced, SSL valid
- Lighthouse on production mirror dev numbers
- Search Console verified, sitemap submitted
- Rollback procedure documented and tested once
- v1.0.0 git tag pushed

title: M7: SEO, OG images, performance audit
description:
Final cross-cutting pass before launch.

SEO:
- astro-seo on every page with title + description from frontmatter
- @astrojs/sitemap configured with i18n filter
- robots.txt allow-all + sitemap reference
- hreflang tags on every page (id, en, x-default)
- JSON-LD: Organization (home), Article (blog), BreadcrumbList (detail), CreativeWork (portfolio)
- Canonical URLs

OG images:
- Auto-generated via Satori for every route
- Template: brand mark + title + bucket/category tag
- Twitter card meta added

Performance:
- Image audit: AVIF + WebP fallback, sized variants, blur placeholders, loading='lazy' below the fold
- Font preload on critical pages
- Inline critical CSS (Astro built-in)
- Lighthouse run on / (id + en), /portfolio, /portfolio/reab, /founder, /blog, /blog/[any post]
- Target: mobile >= 95 perf, 100 a11y, 100 best-practices, 100 SEO

A11y:
- axe-core run on every page; zero violations
- Manual keyboard nav + screen reader pass
- Color contrast verified (lime accent on ink only for text)
acceptance_criteria:
- Lighthouse mobile >= 95/100/100/100 on at least 6 sampled routes
- Sitemap includes all pages, both locales
- OG images render correctly when sharing on Twitter/LinkedIn (manual preview)
- axe-core: zero violations site-wide
- All hreflang pairs validated

title: M5: Services + About + Founder pages
description:
Three long-form static pages, all bilingual.

/services:
- Hero with category overview
- 3 category sections (Customer-facing / Internal ops / Product engineering)
- Each lists: tagline, deliverables bullets, ideal-for bullets
- CTA at bottom

/services/[slug]:
- Per-service detail (e.g., Website & Customer Experience, Mobile App Development, Dashboard & Internal Systems, Membership & Loyalty Systems, Product Discovery & MVP Development, Maintenance & Enhancement)
- Optional FAQ section
- CTA

/about:
- Company narrative (per brief 'Draft about page copy')
- 5 message pillars (Business understanding / Technical credibility / Practical execution / Regional roots / Founder-led trust)
- Small testimonial grid (if any)
- CTA

/founder:
- Hero: founder photo (professional), name 'Fajrian Aidil Pratama', title 'Founder & Director'
- Headline 'Led by engineering experience across product, platform, and operational systems'
- 1–2 paragraph narrative
- Selected experience: FounderExperienceStrip with logos for GoTo Financial, Reku, Stockbit/Bibit, Ulearna, public-sector context (no dates, no titles per brief)
- 5 credibility bullets
- Link out: LinkedIn / GitHub / Medium / YouTube
- CTA: 'Schedule a call'

Author employer logos as monochrome SVG in src/assets/companies/.
acceptance_criteria:
- All pages render in both locales with hreflang
- Founder employer logo strip displays 4 logos with hover-tooltip context
- /services and /services/[slug] both work
- Copy sourced from brief draft sections (founder draft, about draft, service cards)
- Lighthouse a11y >= 100 on /founder
notes:
Service taxonomy expansion (added 2026-05-08):

Banua Coder also offers:
- Design (UI/UX, Logo, Branding) — full design capability beyond just product UI
- Social Media Handling — content + community management

This is strategically critical for the target UMKM partnerships (Kopi Arobi, Kopi 1/3, Elza beauty) who may not need software development immediately but DO need brand + social media work.

Updated service taxonomy for /services (5 categories instead of 3):

A. Customer-facing products
   - Company profile websites
   - Membership apps
   - Mobile apps (iOS/Android/Flutter)
   - Customer portals
   - Landing pages and campaign sites

B. Internal operational systems
   - Attendance systems
   - Approval and workflow systems
   - Reporting dashboards
   - Admin panels
   - Lightweight ERP / internal management tools

C. Digital product & engineering support
   - Product discovery
   - MVP development
   - System modernization
   - UI/UX implementation
   - Maintenance and enhancement

D. Design (NEW)
   - UI/UX design (apps, websites, dashboards)
   - Logo design
   - Brand identity systems
   - Marketing collateral

E. Social Media Management (NEW)
   - Content strategy
   - Content production (graphics, copy, video)
   - Community management
   - Performance reporting

Implementation notes for M5 agent:
- Extend services collection Zod schema enum: 'customer-facing' | 'internal-ops' | 'product-engineering' | 'design' | 'social-media'
- Author 5–6 new service MDX entries for design + social media
- Update home page services preview to surface 5 categories cleanly (consider 2-row card grid or 2 lead categories + 'see all services')
- Founder section should remain engineering-led — clarify Fajrian leads the technical side, with design + social media as in-house team capabilities (don't dilute the engineering positioning, but acknowledge the full-service capability)
- Update ideal-client section on home: F&B / beauty / UMKM clients can engage Banua Coder for brand + social work even without software needs
Team data captured (2026-05-08) from existing https://banuacoder.com/about-us:

src/data/team.ts now contains 4 members:
1. Fajrian Aidil Pratama — Founder & Director (highlight: 'founder')
2. Aryafianto — UI/UX Designer
3. Muh. Zhen — Graphic Designer & Illustrator (covers branding + social media graphics)
4. Arsadi — Frontend Engineer

M5 implementation guidance:
- /about page must include a 'Tim' section with all 4 members rendered as profile cards
- Use the existing FounderCard pattern as inspiration but lighter for non-founder members
- Founder gets extra visual weight (larger photo, longer bio)
- Each card: photo + name + role + 3 expertise tags + social icons
- Photo files go in src/assets/team/ (currently empty — placeholders until real photos provided)
- Founder section on /founder page stays focused on Fajrian alone (engineering credibility)

Positioning decisions confirmed (2026-05-08):
- Home services preview: TWO-TIER supercategories — 'Build & Engineer' and 'Brand & Grow'
  * Build & Engineer covers categories A (customer-facing products), B (internal ops), C (engineering support)
  * Brand & Grow covers categories D (design) and E (social media)
- Founder framing stays 'engineering-led'; team capabilities surfaced on /about
- Service detail pages still cover all 5 categories on /services

title: M4: Portfolio system + 6 case studies
description:
Portfolio index + per-project detail + 6 launch case studies authored bilingually.

Index page (/portfolio, /en/portfolio):
- Bucket filter island (All / Consumer / Public Sector / Operational)
- Card grid sorted by featured + order
- Each card: cover, bucket tag, client, year, summary, hover parallax

Detail page (/portfolio/[slug], /en/portfolio/[slug]):
- Hero cover image
- Metadata strip (client / year / bucket / stack)
- Problem section
- Solution section
- Screenshot gallery wrapped in DeviceFrame components
- Impact bullet list
- External links (Play Store / App Store / web / github)
- Pinned testimonial card if a testimonial references this project
- 'Next case study' link

6 case studies to author (id + en MDX, real screenshots from founder):
- Reab (consumer, Ulearna international, 50k+ downloads)
- Carwa — Digital Car Auction (consumer, Ulearna)
- Caretaker.id (consumer, healthcare)
- Patonro (public-sector, regional tax)
- Lontara (public-sector, regional tax)
- PICO Sulteng (public-sector, COVID-19 platform)

Founder will provide screenshots; until then use anonymized DeviceFrame placeholders.
acceptance_criteria:
- All 6 detail pages render in both locales
- Bucket filter works without page reload (island)
- All images optimized via astro:assets (AVIF + WebP fallback, blur placeholder)
- DeviceFrame component handles phone, tablet, desktop, and blurred (NDA) variants
- Each case study has hreflang alternate links
- Lighthouse on a sample case study mobile >= 95 perf

title: M3: Home page
description:
Build the full home page in both ID and EN. Sections in order:

1. Hero — headline ('Build digital systems that actually help your business grow' / ID equivalent), subheadline, primary + secondary CTA, mock device collage with 3 angled product screens
2. Trust strip — Engineering-led delivery / Cross-context experience / Mobile, web, dashboard / Practical solutions
3. About snippet — partner teknologi narrative
4. Services preview — 3 categories (Customer-facing / Internal ops / Product engineering) with icon + tagline + link
5. Why Banua Coder — 4 differentiators (Business-aware / Engineering-led / Low-risk / Cross-context)
6. Process — 6 steps (Discovery → Solution Mapping → Design & Validation → Development → Testing & Launch → Support & Iteration)
7. Portfolio highlight — 3 featured case studies with bucket tags
8. Founder strip — photo + name + headline + employer logo strip + 'Read more' link
9. Featured testimonial slot (hides if none)
10. Final CTA — 'Konsultasikan Kebutuhan Anda'

Copy is sourced from the brief. Author both id and en MDX in src/content/pages/home.{id,en}.mdx so non-dev edits work.
acceptance_criteria:
- Both / and /en/ render fully populated home with real content
- All sections responsive (320px → 1440px)
- Lighthouse mobile >= 95 perf, 100 a11y, 100 best-practices, 100 SEO
- All links resolve (no 404s in nav)
- Hero loads under 1.8s LCP on slow 4G simulation
- Reduced motion path verified

title: M2: Design system & primitives
description:
Build the core component library and visual primitives. Output is a /\_dev/components review page (excluded from prod build) that renders every component in light + dark, both locales.

Components to ship:
- Layout: Nav, Footer, MobileDrawer (Vue/Svelte island), LangSwitcher (island)
- Typography: SectionHeader (with [ 01 ] bracketed numerals + // mono micro-label), Prose (MDX wrapper)
- Atoms: Button (primary CTA + ghost), Card, Tag, MonoLabel
- Hero: Hero, DotGridBackdrop (parallax, prefers-reduced-motion aware)
- Service: ServiceCard
- Portfolio: CaseStudyCard, BucketFilter (island), DeviceFrame (phone/tablet/desktop variants, blurred variant for NDA)
- Process: ProcessStep
- Founder: FounderCard, FounderExperienceStrip (logo strip with hover context tooltips)
- Trust: TrustStrip, ClientLogoRow (grayscale, color-on-hover)
- Social proof: Testimonial
- CTA: CTA block component
- Theme toggle (island) defaulting to prefers-color-scheme

Setup:
- Geist + Inter + JetBrains Mono via @fontsource, preloaded
- Color tokens locked: --bnc-ink, --bnc-paper, --bnc-accent (#E8FF5A), warm stone scale
- Fluid type scale via clamp()
acceptance_criteria:
- /\_dev/components renders every component in light + dark
- WCAG AA contrast verified on all combos (axe-core or manual)
- prefers-reduced-motion disables parallax + scroll fades
- Mobile drawer opens/closes, traps focus, closes on Esc
- LangSwitcher updates URL correctly
- Lighthouse on /\_dev/components: a11y >= 95
notes:
M2 completed on feature/m2-design-system. 12 commits, 22 components across 9 folders, /dev/components review page (noindex + sitemap-excluded).

Verified:
- pnpm exec astro check: 0 errors
- pnpm run build: green
- Dev page properly noindexed and excluded from sitemap
- WCAG AA, prefers-reduced-motion, dark mode + FOUC prevention all working
- Hand-coded BC monogram SVG (extraction from stamp infeasible due to clip-path complexity)

Known issues for M3:
1. /en/ route does NOT build HTML — agent removed src/pages/en/index.astro thinking i18n fallback would handle it, but Astro static output doesn't generate /en/ redirect HTML automatically. M3 must restore an explicit src/pages/en/index.astro (or pages/[...locale]/index.astro pattern) when building the real home page.
2. Hand-coded icon-only SVG letterforms differ slightly from official wordmark — user should review against PNG1 reference and request adjustments if proportions feel off.
3. Founder photo placeholder still in FounderCard — needs real headshot before M5.
4. Client/company SVG logos still missing — text fallback works but real logos preferred for M3+.

Final M2 git log:

title: M1: Foundation — Astro 5 scaffold, i18n, content collections, design tokens
description:
Scaffold the new Astro project with everything downstream milestones depend on.

Tasks:
- Archive current Laravel codebase to branch legacy/laravel-cms, push, then nuke source tree on develop
- npm create astro@latest with TypeScript strict
- Install: @astrojs/mdx, @astrojs/sitemap, @astrojs/rss, tailwindcss v4, @tailwindcss/vite, @astrojs/check
- Configure astro.config.mjs: site URL, output 'static', i18n { defaultLocale: 'id', locales: ['id','en'], routing: { prefixDefaultLocale: false }, fallback: { en: 'id' } }
- Tailwind v4 setup with CSS custom properties for tokens (--bnc-ink, --bnc-paper, --bnc-accent, etc.)
- Create src/content/config.ts with Zod schemas for portfolio, services, blog, authors, testimonials, pages collections
- Self-host fonts via @fontsource (Geist, Inter, JetBrains Mono)
- Create src/data/clients.ts and src/data/founder-experience.ts stubs
- Create empty src/assets/{clients,companies}/ for logos
- README with dev/build/deploy commands
acceptance_criteria:
- npm run build completes without errors
- Both / (ID) and /en/ render a placeholder page
- Zod schema validation triggers on intentional bad MDX
- Tailwind v4 CSS tokens accessible in components
- Type-check (astro check) passes
notes:
M1 completed on feature/m1-foundation.

Stack landed: Astro 6.3.1 + Tailwind v4.2.4 + pnpm 10.26 + Node 22.16. MDX + sitemap + RSS + zod schemas (6 collections) + i18n (id default, en fallback) + self-hosted fonts (Geist/Inter/JetBrains Mono) + design tokens (--bnc-ink/paper/accent + warm stone scale).

Verification: pnpm exec astro check = 0 errors. pnpm run build = green, both /index.html and /en/index.html generated. Zod validation confirmed via deliberate bad MDX test. No package-lock.json.

Deviations from plan: (1) fonts loaded via CSS @import in global.css instead of JS imports in BaseLayout — cleaner Tailwind v4 idiom; (2) zod import path changed to 'astro/zod' (was 'astro:content') — Astro 6 API change.

Open follow-ups for M2:
- Real SVG logos for src/assets/{clients,companies}/
- Confirm accent color #E8FF5A (lime) before visual review starts
- Upgrade lucide-astro → @lucide/astro (deprecation warning)
- Investigate harmless /en route conflict warning under trailingSlash:'always' i18n

6 commits on this branch; ready for PR to develop after M1 acceptance:
0e75767 chore(m1): remove legacy laravel codebase
6b84fd4 feat(m1): scaffold astro 6 with mdx, sitemap, and tailwind v4
6e02a45 feat(m1): add design tokens, base layout, and bilingual placeholder pages
e1c1a05 feat(m1): add zod schemas and content collection scaffolding
78432d6 feat(m1): add static data stubs and asset dirs
3d89121 docs(m1): replace default readme and finalize gitignore

title: Banua Coder website redesign
description:
Replace the legacy Laravel 8 + Blade CMS template with a modern, static, content-as-code Astro 5 site that positions Banua Coder as an engineering-led technology partner per the brief at docs/banua_coder_company_profile_website_brief.md.

Stack: Astro 5 (static) + Tailwind v4 + MDX content collections + bilingual i18n (ID/EN). Deploy via GitHub Actions + rsync over SSH to the existing shared host. WhatsApp deeplink for contact (no form backend). No data migration from the legacy Laravel CMS.

Full design: docs/plans/2026-05-08-banua-coder-redesign-design.md
acceptance_criteria:
- Live site replaces the current banuacoder.com
- Both ID and EN locales render with hreflang
- All 6 launch case studies (Reab, Carwa, Caretaker.id, Patonro, Lontara, PICO Sulteng) published bilingually with screenshots
- Services index + per-service detail pages
- Founder page with employer logo strip (GoTo Financial, Reku, Stockbit/Bibit, Ulearna)
- Blog scaffolded with at least 1 launch post
- Lighthouse mobile >= 95 on home + 1 case study
- WCAG AA passes
- GitHub Actions deploy on push to main works end-to-end
- Legacy Laravel codebase archived to legacy/laravel-cms branch

title: M5.7: Long-form content + Unsplash stock imagery across services / about / founder
description:
Expand main content across the three deepest informational pages and add Unsplash imagery for visual richness. Decisions locked: source = Unsplash (free commercial, no attribution required); apply to /services + /about + /founder + service detail pages.

SCOPE:

A. /services index — add per-supercategory intro narrative + supporting Unsplash imagery.
   - 'Build & Engineer' supergroup: 2-3 paragraph intro framing software/systems work; 1 Unsplash image (engineering / dashboards / mobile development theme)
   - 'Brand & Grow' supergroup: 2-3 paragraph intro framing design + social media work; 1 Unsplash image (design studio / creative process theme)
   - Engagement-models section at the bottom: project-based / discovery sprint / retainer / managed — 4 cards with 1-line descriptions

B. /services/[slug] detail pages — for each of the 8 services, expand body MDX:
   - Currently 2-3 paragraphs → expand to 5-7 paragraphs
   - Sections: Why this matters / What we deliver / How we work / Ideal for / FAQ
   - 1 supporting Unsplash image per detail page (relevant theme)
   - Keep deliverables and idealFor lists tight

C. /about — expand company narrative
   - Current: 2 paragraphs + 5 message pillars + team grid
   - Expand to: hero with eyebrow + 1-paragraph story / 4-5 paragraph 'Our story' section / 5 message pillars (kept as-is) / 'How we work' visual sequence with 3-4 stages + Unsplash imagery / team grid (kept) / values section / 'Why work with us' close
   - 2-3 Unsplash images: founders working / dashboard demo / team collaboration

D. /founder — longer founder story
   - Current: hero + 1 narrative paragraph + employer strip + selected experience + 5 credibility bullets + selected work product
   - Expand to: hero / 3-4 paragraph 'About Fajrian' story / employer strip (kept) / 'Cross-context execution' deeper section / 'Engineering philosophy' section / 'Selected work product' (kept) / 'Connect / Learn more' links section
   - 1-2 Unsplash images: workspace / engineering craft (subtle, NOT stock-portrait of a random person)

E. UNSPLASH IMAGE WORKFLOW:
   - Use Unsplash Source URLs at build time OR download via curl during a one-time setup
   - Save to src/assets/stock/ with descriptive names (e.g., engineering-team-1.jpg, design-studio-1.jpg)
   - Astro asset pipeline handles optimization (AVIF/WebP/sized variants)
   - Pick 8-10 images total: keep visual cohesion (similar warm tones, soft natural light, avoid generic 'business meeting' cliche)
   - Each image: include unsplash.com/photos/xxx URL in a comment for attribution audit

F. CONTENT QUALITY GUARDRAILS:
   - Don't pad — if a section reads tighter at 3 paragraphs than 5, keep 3
   - Match brand voice: confident, practical, engineering-led, not vendor-y
   - Avoid 'AI solutions' / 'digital transformation' filler
   - Keep both ID and EN locales in sync
   - Quote real founder language where the brief provides it
acceptance_criteria:
- /services has 2 supergroup intros + 4-card engagement section
- 8 service detail pages have 5-7 paragraph bodies + 1 image each
- /about has expanded narrative including 'Our story' + 'How we work' sections + 2-3 images
- /founder has 3-4 paragraph story + engineering philosophy section + 1-2 ambient images
- Both ID and EN locales updated consistently
- Each Unsplash image is downloaded to src/assets/stock/ (not runtime CDN)
- pnpm exec astro check 0 errors
- pnpm run build green
- Lighthouse delta from M4.5 baseline: ≤3 perf points

title: M4.1: Additional portfolio entries (Detexi, Ayana, Siap Donggala, BPS suite)
description:
Expand the portfolio with additional projects that surfaced after M4 was already in flight. Group into appropriate buckets and decide on a new bucket for SDK/engineering work.

CONSUMER / DIGITAL PRODUCTS bucket — add:
- Detexi / Detexi Doctor (healthcare digital product, alongside Caretaker.id)
- Ayana / ayana_web (need to clarify with founder: consumer-facing or operational?)

PUBLIC SECTOR bucket — add:
- Siap Donggala (Mekari Talenta-based HR/talent management for Pemda Donggala — Donggala regency, Sulawesi Tengah)
- BPS Performance Metrics (internal tool for Badan Pusat Statistik staff performance)
- BPS SE Scrapper (Sensus Ekonomi data scraper / collection tool)

NEW BUCKET — Engineering / SDKs / Tooling:
- bps_sso_sdk (SSO library for BPS ecosystem apps)
- form_gear_engine_sdk (form rendering engine SDK)
- form_gear_builder (visual form builder)
This new bucket frames technical-depth credibility separately from product case studies. Update the bucket Zod enum: 'consumer' | 'public-sector' | 'operational' | 'engineering'. Update BucketFilter and CaseStudyCard tag styling.

EXCLUDE from public portfolio:
- Sikerja (unreleased — may add later when launched, or keep private)

Author each as bilingual MDX with same structure as M4's 6 launch case studies (problem / solution / impact / stack / links). For SDKs/libraries, the 'links' should point to GitHub repo URLs if open source. For BPS internal tools, may be limited to anonymized descriptions if proprietary.

Confirm with founder before publishing:
- Which BPS work is OK to mention publicly (some may be NDA-restricted)
- Ayana: consumer or operational classification
- Siap Donggala: gov-side public mention OK?
- Detexi: client name OK to publish?
acceptance_criteria:
- Bucket Zod enum extended to 4 values
- BucketFilter shows 4 buckets, all functional
- 5–8 new case studies authored (depending on what founder approves for public)
- New 'engineering' bucket renders distinctively from product buckets
- Founder approval logged for each new entry
- /portfolio renders all entries cleanly with the expanded filter set

title: M5.6: Credibility audit for target audiences (F&B, beauty, government)
description:
Review and tune the marketing site copy + structure to land credible for the immediate partnership targets:

- Kopi Arobi (F&B / coffee, UMKM)
- Kopi 1/3 by brkh (F&B / coffee, UMKM)
- Elza beauty skin (beauty / skincare, UMKM)
- Pemkot Palu (city government, Palu)
- Pemprov Sulteng (province government, Sulawesi Tengah)

Two distinct audiences:
A. UMKM F&B / beauty: need approachable + premium tone, business-outcome language ('rapikan operasional', 'tingkatkan repeat order', 'membership system'), local relatable case studies
B. Government / institutional: need formal + trust language, public-sector capability proof (PICO Sulteng, Patonro, Lontara already showcased), structured process visibility, security/reliability signals

Audit pass:
- /services: ensure category descriptions name-check both F&B-relevant and gov-relevant use cases
- /portfolio: confirm public-sector bucket leads with PICO/Patonro/Lontara so government visitors see proof immediately
- /about: tone reads consultative, not vendor-y
- /founder: surface 'cross-context execution' (consumer + public + international) prominently — this is the strongest angle for both audiences
- Industries / ideal-client section on home: explicitly mention F&B, retail, beauty, organisasi/instansi, sektor publik
- Social proof: when these partnerships land, add their logos + a one-line testimonial
- Government-friendly trust signals: 'Engineering-led delivery', 'experience with regional government workflows', 'data + payment domain experience'

Deliverables:
- Edits across home / about / services / founder / portfolio MDX
- Document changes in commit messages
- Add a section on home: 'Cocok untuk' (Ideal client fit) bilingual
acceptance_criteria:
- All target audiences (UMKM F&B + beauty + government) have explicit copy hooks somewhere on the site
- Portfolio bucket framing leads with public-sector for government readers
- Home explicitly names F&B / retail / beauty / instansi / pemerintah daerah in the ideal-client-fit section
- Founder section prominently mentions cross-context (public + consumer + international) execution
- No condescending or generic vendor language

title: M5.5: App project estimation calculator (/estimate)
description:
Lead-generation calculator that lets visitors get a rough scope/budget estimate for an app or system project. Output is a range (IDR low — high) plus a context line, leading directly to a WhatsApp CTA prefilled with their answers.

Page: /estimate (id) + /en/estimate (en). Linked from nav, services page, and final CTA on home.

UX: 5–7 step wizard, one question per screen, progress bar, keyboard navigable.

Inputs (all client-side, no backend):
1. Project type: Mobile app (iOS/Android/Flutter) | Web app/dashboard | Internal operational system | Website/landing | Hybrid/multi-platform
2. Scale: Single feature MVP | Standard product | Multi-module platform
3. Users: Internal only | Customer-facing public | Mixed
4. Critical features (multi-select, weighted): Auth + roles, Payments, Real-time / chat, Offline support, Push notifications, External API integration, Admin panel, Reporting dashboard, Multi-language, E-sign, File handling
5. Design level: Use templates / minimal | Custom UX/UI | Full design system + branding
6. Timeline urgency: Standard (4-12 weeks) | Aggressive (rush)
7. Maintenance: One-time delivery | Ongoing engagement

Output:
- Range in IDR with low/high (transparent: 'estimasi awal, bukan quotation final')
- Suggested team composition + duration
- 3 example past projects from portfolio that match the profile
- Strong CTA: 'Diskusikan estimasi ini di WhatsApp' — deeplink prefilled with their selections summary
- Secondary: 'Save your answers' (downloads JSON)

Implementation:
- Client-side TypeScript (vanilla, no framework needed)
- State persisted in URL hash (so back button works + shareable)
- Pricing model in src/data/estimator-pricing.ts (configurable, easy to tune)
- Calculator logic in src/lib/estimator.ts (testable function)
- Component: src/components/sections/EstimatorWizard.astro

Disclaimer copy: this gives a rough range only; final scope/quotation requires a discovery call.
acceptance_criteria:
- /estimate and /en/estimate render with full wizard flow
- All 7 steps work, keyboard accessible
- Output range is reasonable (cross-checked against industry rates IDR 25–300M typical project range)
- WhatsApp deeplink prefilled correctly with user inputs summarized
- No backend, no analytics on inputs (privacy-first)
- Disclaimer prominent in output
- Linked from nav, services, and home final CTA
- Lighthouse mobile ≥ 95 on /estimate

title: M4.5: Micro-interactions + scroll-based animations
description:
Add restrained, premium micro-interactions across the site to boost engagement without compromising the engineering-led editorial tone. Schedule AFTER M6 so all sections exist, BEFORE M7 (SEO/perf pass) so the perf audit catches any regressions.

Scope (all respect prefers-reduced-motion):
- Scroll-revealed fades for section content via IntersectionObserver (200ms, once-only)
- Hero parallax dot-grid (already in DotGridBackdrop component, verify works on home)
- Magnetic-style CTA hover for primary buttons (subtle, ~6px translate towards cursor)
- Stagger-in animation on card grids (services, portfolio cards) when section enters viewport
- Hover lift on case study cards (M2 already shipped this — verify polish)
- Smooth scroll for in-page anchor links
- Animated counter for any numbers in trust strip / process / impact stats (count-up on viewport enter)
- Cyan accent stripe sweep-in on link hover underline
- Page transition: subtle fade between routes (Astro view transitions API)
- Theme toggle icon morph (sun ↔ moon)

Target: zero reflows during scroll, no animation longer than 600ms, total perf budget +10ms TBT max.
acceptance_criteria:
- All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion
- Lighthouse mobile perf delta from M3 baseline: ≤5 points
- No layout shift introduced (CLS unchanged)
- All animations work in both light and dark mode
- View transitions API works between routes
- Counter animations only trigger on viewport enter
- Founder visual review: feels premium, not gimmicky

title: BO-M8: Production deploy + backup + handover
description: Deploy to app.banuacoder.com. Daily DB backup to S3 / R2 / B2. SSL. Cron schedules (dunning, recurring summaries). Sentry/Bugsnag error tracking. Founder onboarding doc. Disaster recovery test.

title: BO-M7: Multi-language UI + branded PDF templates
description: Admin UI in ID + EN (Filament has i18n). Client-facing emails + PDF templates (invoice, quotation, contract) in client's locale. Banua Coder logo on every PDF. Brand colors from the marketing site palette.

title: BO-M6: Accounting + Indonesian tax (PPH Badan)
description: Income/expense ledger with categories. Monthly summary report exportable as CSV/PDF for accountant: gross income, PPH 23 withheld (2% jasa), PPN out (11%), PPN in (11% from supplier), PPH 21 (if any contractors). Annual summary for SPT Tahunan PPH Badan. Bank account reconciliation (manual import or Mandiri/BCA API if accessible). Profit/loss statement.

title: BO-M5: Contract editor + e-sign
description: Tiptap rich-text editor with template variables (client name, project, dates, fees). Contract templates library. Versioning + diff. Public signing page with audit log (IP, timestamp, browser, screenshot). Optional DocuSeal integration. PDF export.

title: BO-M4: Invoice + Xendit + Lemon Squeezy
description: Invoice CRUD with line items + tax handling (PPN 11%, PPH 23 2% withholding). Status: draft / sent / partially-paid / paid / overdue / cancelled. Xendit integration for IDR (virtual account, e-wallet, retail outlets). Lemon Squeezy for USD international as MoR. Webhooks for payment events. Dunning emails for overdue. PDF export. Public payment link.

title: BO-M3: Quotation workflow
description: Create quotation (line items, taxes, discounts, terms). Send via email with public accept/reject link. Track status (draft/sent/accepted/rejected/expired). Convert accepted quotation into invoice. Versioning. PDF export via Spatie Browsershot.

title: BO-M2: Client management
description: Client CRUD (name, type, NPWP, address, contacts, primary contact). Contact log (timeline of emails/calls/notes). File attachments. Search + filter.

title: BO-M1: Foundation — Laravel 12 + auth + Filament + deploy skeleton
description: Scaffold Laravel 12, install Filament v3, set up auth, configure DB (PostgreSQL preferred), create deploy skeleton on app.banuacoder.com subdomain. Decide DB host (same shared host or managed service). Configure SSH deploy via GitHub Actions similar to marketing site. Pin pnpm equivalent (composer + npm/pnpm for Filament assets).

title: Banua Coder back-office (app.banuacoder.com)
description:
Separate Laravel 12 app at app.banuacoder.com for internal business operations. Refrens-style: clients, quotations, invoices, payments (Xendit + Lemon Squeezy), contracts with inline editing + e-sign, plus Indonesian accounting for PPH Badan / PPN / PPH 23 monthly summaries.

Architectural decision: separate app from the Astro marketing site at banuacoder.com. Marketing stays static + CDN-friendly; back-office gets full server runtime, DB, payment webhooks, PDF generation.

Stack proposal:
- Laravel 12 + Filament v3 (admin UI; CRUD-heavy + auth out-of-the-box) OR Laravel + Inertia + Vue 3
- PostgreSQL or MySQL
- Spatie Browsershot for PDF (invoices, quotations, contracts)
- Tiptap rich-text editor for contract bodies (versioning + variables)
- DocuSeal for e-sign OR custom click-to-sign with audit log + IP/timestamp
- Xendit for IDR payments (Indonesia-first), Lemon Squeezy for USD international (MoR)
- Postmark / Mailgun / SES for transactional email
- Tax: PPH 23 (2% jasa), PPN (11%), PPH 21 monthly summaries, exportable for accountant

Sequencing: starts AFTER marketing site M3-M8 ships.
acceptance_criteria:
- Live at app.banuacoder.com with HTTPS
- Auth: founder + future team logins
- Client CRUD with contact log
- Quotation create/send/accept/convert-to-invoice
- Invoice + payment via Xendit (IDR) and Lemon Squeezy (USD)
- Contract create with rich-text editor + public sign link + audit log
- Monthly accounting summary (income/expense/PPH 23/PPN) exportable as CSV/PDF for accountant
- Indonesian + English UI and PDF templates
- Backup strategy in place
- Founder onboarded and using the system

title: M6: Blog + Testimonials + Contact
description:
Three independent surfaces.

Blog:
- /blog index — card grid paginated 12 per page, sorted by publishedAt desc
- /blog/[slug] — article layout (Prose component) with author bio strip at end
- /rss.xml feed
- Tag filter island (optional, can defer)
- 1 launch post drafted in MDX (announcement + positioning piece)

Testimonials:
- src/content/testimonials/ collection wired
- Featured testimonial rotator on home (above final CTA, hides if empty)
- Pinned testimonial card on portfolio detail when project reference matches
- Small testimonial grid section on /about
- Empty-state-friendly: ship with zero, add later via MDX edit only

Contact:
- /contact and /en/contact
- Headline + intro per brief
- Primary CTA: WhatsApp deeplink (wa.me/[number]) with prefilled message:
  'Halo Banua Coder, saya tertarik untuk konsultasi mengenai [project type].'
- Secondary: email (mailto:), Instagram, LinkedIn, GitHub
- Office address + simple embedded map (or static map image to avoid Google Maps JS)
- Hours of operation
acceptance_criteria:
- /blog renders with at least 1 launch post
- RSS feed validates
- Testimonials collection empty by default; site builds clean
- WhatsApp deeplink prefills correctly on mobile + desktop
- Map renders without external JS (or async-load only)
- All bilingual
